sn12.45:1.1: Thus have I heard. On one occasion the Blessed One was dwelling at Ñātika in the Brick Hall. Then, while the Blessed One was alone in seclusion, he uttered this Dhamma exposition:
sn12.45:2.1: "In dependence on the eye and forms, eye-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feelingcomes to be; with feeling as condition, craving; with craving as condition, clinging … . Such is the origin of this whole mass of suffering.
sn12.45:3.1: "In dependence on the ear and sounds … In dependence on the mind and mental phenomena, mind-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feelingcomes to be; with feeling as condition, craving; with craving as condition, clinging … . Such is the origin of this whole mass of suffering.
sn12.45:4.1: "In dependence on the eye and forms, eye-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feelingcomes to be; with feeling as condition, craving. But with the remainderless fading away and cessation of that same craving comes cessation of clinging; with the cessation of clinging, cessation of existence … . Such is the cessation of this whole mass of suffering.sn.ii.75
sn12.45:5.1: "In dependence on the ear and sounds … In dependence on the mind and mental phenomena, mind-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feelingcomes to be; with feeling as condition, craving. But with the remainderless fading away and cessation of that same craving comes cessation of clinging; with the cessation of clinging, cessation of existence … . Such is the cessation of this whole mass of suffering."
sn12.45:6.1: Now on that occasion a certain bhikkhu was standing listening in on the Blessed One. The Blessed One saw him standing there listening in and said to him: "Did you hear that Dhamma exposition, bhikkhu?"
sn12.45:6.3: "Learn that Dhamma exposition, bhikkhu, master it and remember it. That Dhamma exposition is beneficial and relevant to the fundamentals of the holy life."
